commit.message:
r600: CPU Starvation Theorem formalization and per-benchmark RCA

Formal findings from triple RCA investigation on AMD E-300 APU:

CPU Starvation Theorem:
- IPC = 0.21 (79% stalled), 27% L2 cache miss, 21% branch mispredict
- GPU utilization 70.83% composite; sh% == gpu% everywhere (GPU is
  shader-busy when fed, all idle time is CPU starvation)
- ta% = 0% on all benchmarks including texture — TEX fetch is never
  the bottleneck
- Five starvation mechanisms identified (DRM ioctl, GEM_WAIT_IDLE,
  Bobcat pipeline stalls, LTO code bloat, thermal coupling)
- Six testable hypotheses with expected outcomes

Per-Benchmark RCA:
- shading:phong 635 FPS (GPU 66%) — signal wait 1.3ms (82% of frame)
- texture 808 FPS (GPU 51%) — CPU-starved, GPU idle 49%
- vertex 771 FPS (GPU 68%) — highest VGT util, 32% starvation gap
- Theoretical ceilings: 962 / 1584 / 1134 FPS at 100% GPU utilization

Submit time regression (70-95µs vs prior 10-18µs target):
- Root cause: added BO references, conservative flush packets,
  alloca/memcpy overhead from fence elision infrastructure
- Recovery: shader suballoc, descriptor consolidation, persistent ring

Data archived at:
  x130e:/home/eirikr/workspaces/mesa/maps/telemetry/per_benchmark_gpu_rca_20260410/

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

v0.3.5: persistent scraper health monitoring in run script

commit.message:
fix(simulate-pipeline): swap tfvars for TFLint step to avoid template syntax errors

TFLint auto-loads terraform.tfvars. In the skeleton/ directory this file
contains Backstage template syntax (${{values.*}}) which is not valid HCL,
causing TFLint to fail with 'Invalid character' errors.

Fix: temporarily replace terraform.tfvars with tests/test.tfvars (which has
real values) before running TFLint, then restore the original. This mirrors
what happens in the actual pipeline where the file is already rendered.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: Morpheus final review & merge of PR #411 (Paparazzi tests) and PR #412 (Maestro E2E flows)

Summary of merged PRs:
- PR #411: 4 Paparazzi screenshot tests (History, Settings, Programs, Profile) = 14 test cases, 588 lines
- PR #412: 5 Maestro E2E flows (RPE entry, plan day detail, custom exercise, settings, home quick-start) = 732 lines, coverage 28→33 flows

Both PRs validated against architectural principles:
- Test pyramid correctness (screenshot layer separate from E2E layer)
- Comprehensive critical path coverage
- Consistent patterns and code quality
- No boundary violations or tech debt

Sprint board cleared; test infrastructure optimized for next iteration.

commit.message:
refactor: rename sp-crossplane/CROSSPLANE_SP → sp-platform/PLATFORM_SP

The bootstrap service principal is not exclusively used for Crossplane —
it also authenticates Terraform runs, the ADO service connection, and the
WIF federated credential. Renaming to sp-platform (per-project) reflects
this broader role and mirrors the mi-{project}-platform naming convention.

Renames applied:
- Env vars:   BOOTSTRAP_SP_CLIENT_ID/SECRET/APP_OBJECT_ID → PLATFORM_SP_*
- TF var:     bootstrap_sp_app_object_id → platform_sp_app_object_id
- TF_VAR_:   TF_VAR_bootstrap_sp_app_object_id → TF_VAR_platform_sp_app_object_id
- Comments:   sp-crossplane / sp_crossplane → sp-platform / sp_platform

Files changed:
  scripts/simulate-pipeline.sh
  scripts/.env.smoketest
  scripts/.env.smoketest.template
  skeleton/terraform/variables.tf
  skeleton/terraform/terraform.tfvars
  skeleton/terraform/ado.tf
  skeleton/terraform/azure.tf
  skeleton/terraform/providers.tf
  skeleton/terraform/tests/test.tfvars
  skeleton/azure-pipelines.yml
  template.yaml

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: avoid OSError 'argument list too long' in CopilotRunner

Write the prompt to a NamedTemporaryFile and pass @<path> to copilot's
--prompt flag instead of inlining the raw text. This sidesteps the OS
ARG_MAX limit which is easily exceeded when prompts include large spec
and plan documents. The temp file is deleted in a finally block whether
the run succeeds or fails.

Bump version to 1.0.0rc6.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: add railway.toml to override npm ci with npm install

Railway may have migrated from Nixpacks to Railpack, making
nixpacks.toml ignored. Added railway.toml with installCommand
to ensure npm install is used instead of npm ci.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Re-launch installer with -NoProfile to avoid import errors

When a user re-installs, their profile may already contain
Import-Module Cicada which fails before the module files are
copied. The installer now detects this and re-launches itself
with -NoProfile via an env-var guard. Also fixes duplicate
profile lines by removing the leading newline from Add-Content.

commit.message:
r600: audit docs vs x130e code; update stale GPR spill and PV/PS claims

Audited SFN sources on x130e (mesa-26-debug at 568fc1deea0) against
canonical docs. Key findings:

- GPR_SPILL_DESIGN.md claimed 'DESIGN PHASE' / 'no spill fallback' but
  register_allocation_with_spill() is fully implemented and wired in at
  sfn_nir.cpp:1013. Updated status to PHASE 1 IMPLEMENTED.
- scheduler_model.md claimed spill is 'design target'. Updated to reflect
  Phase 1 implementation with Phase 2 targets listed.
- SFN_GAP_ANALYSIS.md had inconsistency: Law 1 (PV/PS) listed as PARTIAL
  in main table but FIXED in Appendix B. Updated main table to FIXED.
  Gap 2 (PV/PS scheduling-time optimization) marked RESOLVED.
- All other claims verified: KCACHE (Law 3), register constants,
  read-port validation (Law 4), Terakan SFN bridge, disk cache,
  dynamic state, terakan_sched.h Phase 0 contract.

New finding document: AUDIT_CODE_VS_DOCS_20260412.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix: Load seeder directly using require_once to bypass autoloader issues

Some production environments have issues with PSR-4 autoloading for tenant seeders. Using require_once and direct file loading ensures compatibility.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(providers): remove hardcoded use_oidc from azuread provider

azuread provider had use_oidc = true hardcoded, which broke local SP auth
in simulate-pipeline.sh — with no ARM_OIDC_TOKEN available locally, the
provider could not authenticate.

Fix: remove use_oidc from the provider block entirely. Auth mode is now
controlled by environment variables only:
- Pipeline (WIF/OIDC): ARM_USE_OIDC=true + ARM_OIDC_TOKEN set by pipeline steps
- Local (SP):          ARM_CLIENT_ID + ARM_CLIENT_SECRET set by simulate-pipeline.sh

The azuread provider v3 reads ARM_USE_OIDC from env automatically, so the
pipeline behaviour is unchanged. Both providers (azurerm and azuread) now
use the same env-var-driven auth pattern.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(simulate-pipeline): update for platform MI and bootstrap SP changes

- Remove MI_CROSSPLANE_* variables — platform MI is now created by Terraform
  (azurerm_user_assigned_identity.platform in azure.tf) so no pre-existing MI
  needs to be supplied as input
- Rename CROSSPLANE_SP_* → BOOTSTRAP_SP_* for clarity (the SP is per-project
  and not exclusively used for Crossplane)
- Add BOOTSTRAP_SP_APP_OBJECT_ID — App Registration Object ID needed by
  TF var bootstrap_sp_app_object_id for WIF federated credential creation
- Add missing -var flags to terraform plan and destroy: aks_admin_group_object_id,
  bootstrap_sp_app_object_id, ado_pat — these override the Backstage template
  syntax placeholders that terraform.tfvars contains in skeleton/
- Fix MI_NAME to mi-{project}-platform (matches azurerm_user_assigned_identity.platform)
- Replace hardcoded MI_CROSSPLANE_CLIENT_ID validation with terraform output lookup
  (mi_client_id) so the script is not dependent on a pre-known MI client ID
- Update .env.smoketest.template to match the new variable names and remove stale MI vars

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: CORS proxy security + auto-continue middleware (#34, #37) (#97)

* feat: add CORS proxy endpoints and auto-continue middleware (fixes #34, fixes #37)

## CORS Proxy (#34)
- Add host allowlist validation to arm-proxy, github-proxy, pricing-proxy
- Create github-oauth proxy endpoint for GitHub OAuth device flow
- Shared proxy-allowlist.ts lib with per-route host validation

## Auto-Continue (#37)
- Add auto-continue middleware that detects finish_reason: length
- Automatically sends continuation requests (up to 3 rounds)
- Integrated into converse (streaming + non-streaming) and playground
- chatCompletionWithAutoContinue wrapper for direct use

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: auto-continue retry bound and OAuth path allowlist

- Change loop condition from round <= maxRounds to round < maxRounds
  to prevent one extra continuation call beyond the configured max.
- Add ALLOWED_PATHS set to github-oauth proxy, restricting POST
  forwarding to login/device/code and login/oauth/access_token only.
- Redact internal error details from 502 response body.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: MnWorkflowOrchestrator — generic real-time workflow visualization (#59)

New Maranello component for visualizing any workflow topology in
real-time with animated edges, status transitions, and phase tracking.

Features:
- 4 layout algorithms: circular, horizontal, vertical, auto (force-directed)
- Real-time status updates with smooth CSS/SVG transitions
  (idle → active → thinking → done → error)
- Animated particles on active edges
- Phase/progress indicator bar
- Per-node customization: color, icon, sublabel, badge, group
- Per-edge customization: directed arrows, labels, bidirectional
- CVA size variants (sm/md/lg/fluid)
- Accessible: role=img, aria-label, keyboard-focusable nodes
- i18n via useLocale('workflowOrchestrator')
- Zero external dependencies — pure SVG + CSS animations

Files:
- mn-workflow-orchestrator.tsx (107 lines) — main component
- mn-workflow-orchestrator.helpers.tsx (185 lines) — SVG sub-components
- mn-workflow-orchestrator.layout.ts (162 lines) — layout algorithms
- mn-workflow-orchestrator.types.ts (88 lines) — TypeScript interfaces

Also: added to catalog, barrel exports, and i18n system.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Replace explicit drop() calls with scoping blocks

Refactor 27 drop() calls across 16 files to use scoping blocks instead,
making the lifetime of guards and temporaries visible through code structure
rather than relying on explicit drop() calls.

Categories of replacements:
- Lock guards (read/write): extract needed data from a scope block
- Weak→upgrade patterns: scope the upgrade attempt
- File handles: scope create+write+sync sequences
- Miscellaneous temporaries: bare scope blocks

Preserved drop() calls where scoping is inappropriate:
- Channel sender drops (broadcast close semantics)
- Arc::try_unwrap prerequisites
- Gzip encoder finish() sequences
- Background thread destructors
- Drops in else branches of if-let patterns

Closes #1134

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[cDAC] Add typed field extension methods for Data classes (#126713)

## Summary

Add typed field extension methods for the cDAC managed reader and
convert all `IData<T>` classes to use them. This builds on the native
data descriptor type system (#126163) to add managed-side type
validation.

### Changes

**`TargetFieldExtensions.cs`** — New extension methods on `Target`:
- `ReadField<T>` — reads a primitive field with `Debug.Assert` type
validation
- `ReadPointerField` / `ReadPointerFieldOrNull` — reads pointer fields
(required/optional)
- `ReadNUIntField` — reads native unsigned integer fields
- `ReadCodePointerField` — reads code pointer fields
- `ReadDataField<T>` / `ReadDataFieldPointer<T>` — reads
inline/pointer-to Data struct fields
- `ReadFieldOrDefault<T>` — reads optional primitive fields with default
value

When the data descriptor includes type information (debug/checked
builds), these methods assert that the declared field type is compatible
with the C# read type.

**All ~130 `IData<T>` classes converted** to use the new extension
methods, replacing direct `target.Read<T>(address +
(ulong)type.Fields[...].Offset)` calls.

### Bugs found and fixed

The type system caught several pre-existing issues:
1. **DynamicStaticsInfo.GCStatics/NonGCStatics** — descriptor said
`uint32`, native type is `TADDR` (pointer)
2. **EEClass.FieldDescList** — used `Read<ulong>` for a pointer field
(broken on 32-bit)
3. **ExceptionClause.ClassToken** — read `TypeHandle` (nuint) as `uint`
4. **SimpleComCallWrapper.RefCount** — property was `ulong`, native is
`LONGLONG` (signed)
5. **NativeCodeVersionNode.NativeCode** — descriptor was `T_POINTER`,
actually `CodePointer`
6. **MethodTable.EEClassOrCanonMT** — descriptor was `T_NUINT`, managed
reads as pointer

> [!NOTE]
> This content was generated with the assistance of GitHub Copilot.

---------

Co-authored-by: Max Charlamb <maxcharlamb@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(wasm): unlock select parity via cooperative scheduler

Implements hew_select_first and hew_reply_wait in the WASM reply-channel
module (reply_channel_wasm.rs) and removes the five hew.select.* entries
from the codegen WASM unsupported-op table.

Problem
-------
hew.select.* ops were unconditionally rejected when targeting wasm32.
The rejection reason ("requires OS threads for blocking") was no longer
accurate: the cooperative ask/reply machinery and the tick-based scheduler
(hew_wasm_sched_tick) already existed and were sufficient to implement
select parity without OS threads.

Implementation
--------------
* hew_reply_wait (WASM): drives the cooperative scheduler one activation
  at a time via hew_wasm_sched_tick(1) until the reply channel becomes
  ready, then returns the value. Returns null if the run queue drains
  before a reply arrives (program-level stall).

* hew_select_first (WASM): scans channels in a loop, yielding one
  scheduler activation between each scan. Returns the index of the
  first ready channel, or -1 when the queue drains with no winner.

Fail-closed invariants preserved
---------------------------------
* Finite timeout (timeout_ms >= 0) is NOT supported. WASI clock_time_get
  is not guaranteed in all sandboxes; faking elapsed time would be
  semantically incorrect. hew_select_first returns -1 immediately for
  any non-negative timeout_ms.
  WASM-TODO: non-zero timeout requires WASI clock_time_get support

* Winner-arm selection: first scan after dispatch finds the lowest-index
  ready channel, matching native behaviour.

* Loser cancellation: hew.select.destroy lowers to hew_reply_channel_free
  (unchanged); the channel is marked cancelled before the loser actor
  processes its message, so hew_reply on a cancelled channel is a clean
  no-op (existing WASM implementation).

* No leaked reply channels: the scheduler drains the loser actor's message
  at hew_sched_shutdown(), which calls hew_reply on the cancelled channel
  and releases the sender-side reference cleanly.

Tests
-----
* 10 unit tests in reply_channel_wasm::tests covering: null inputs,
  single ready channel, multi-channel first-wins, finite-timeout
  fail-closed, and empty-queue stall returns -1.
* New WASM e2e test wasm_select_basic.hew proves end-to-end:
  infinite-wait select on WASM, first arm wins deterministically (FIFO
  cooperative queue), loser cleanup leaves no crash/leak.
* Registered in CMakeLists.txt as add_wasm_file_test(select_basic ...).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(wasm-select): resolve timeoutMs sentinel, reject timed multi-arm select, clear spurious WASM warning

- MLIRGenExpr.cpp: default timeoutMs from INT32_MAX to -1 so no-timeout
  WASM selects take the infinite-wait cooperative-scheduler path instead
  of triggering the fail-closed timeout_ms>=0 guard in hew_select_first

- MLIRGenExpr.cpp: add early WASM rejection for timed multi-arm select
  (before any hew.select.add emissions) so asks are never enqueued when
  a timeout is present — avoids orphaned asks after timeout fires
  WASM-TODO: multi-arm timed select deferred until WASI clock_time_get

- hew-types/src/check/expressions.rs: stop emitting WasmUnsupportedFeature
  warning for no-timeout selects; they are now fully supported via the
  cooperative scheduler (hew_select_first / hew_reply_wait). Timed multi-arm
  selects still warn (codegen will then hard-error with clear WASM-TODO message).

- hew-types/src/check/types.rs: update Select warning reason text to
  accurately describe the remaining limitation (multi-arm timed select
  requires WASI clock_time_get) rather than the old OS-thread rationale.

- CMakeLists.txt: register add_wasm_reject_test(select_timed ...) for the
  new wasm_reject_select_timed.hew test that verifies the codegen error path

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(clippy): add safety comments, fix doc backtick, suppress cast lints in wasm select tests

- Add SAFETY comments to all unsafe blocks in the new hew_select_first /
  hew_reply_wait test functions (clippy::undocumented_unsafe_blocks)
- Wrap WASI clock_time_get in backticks in the doc comment (clippy::doc_markdown)
- Add #[expect(cast_possible_truncation/wrap)] with reason on the one test
  site that casts slice len to i32 (the test array is always tiny)
- Reorder SAFETY comment to immediately precede the unsafe block so clippy
  recognises it (the comment must not be interrupted by #[expect] attributes)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: hew

commit.message:
fix: remove azuread_application data source, use bootstrap_sp_app_object_id variable

data.azuread_application requires Application.Read.All Graph API permission
which sp-crossplane doesn't have — resulted in 403 Forbidden during plan.

Instead, accept the app registration Object ID as an explicit variable
bootstrap_sp_app_object_id and construct the resource ID directly as
/applications/{object_id}. No Graph API read needed.

Changes:
- ado.tf: drop data.azuread_application.sp_crossplane; update federated
  credential application_id to /applications/${var.bootstrap_sp_app_object_id}
- variables.tf: add bootstrap_sp_app_object_id variable
- terraform.tfvars: add Backstage template value placeholder
- tests/test.tfvars: add test value
- tests/*.tftest.hcl: remove now-unused mock_data azuread_application block
- template.yaml: add bootstrap_sp_app_object_id field (required, UUID pattern)
  in Azure Configuration step; wire into fetch:template values

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: restore dev/apex build with vp build and hono/vercel adapter

The previous fix commit (36bc46a) accidentally broke the Vercel build by
replacing 'vp build' with 'tsc --noEmit', which produces no output.
Vercel requires a 'dist' directory after the build step.

Restore:
- build script to 'vp build' (via vite-plus)
- vite.config.ts (lib build targeting src/index.ts → dist/index.js)
- hono/vercel handle() adapter in src/index.ts
- vite and vite-plus devDependencies
- tsconfig.json include for vite.config.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add interactive prompt to auto-add Cicada to PowerShell profile

Asks user if they want Import-Module Cicada added to their $PROFILE
after install. Detects if already present, creates profile file if
needed, defaults to Yes on empty input.
